findings.name: data_leakage
findings.headline: 1 feature(s) carry unusually high Impact with low Non-linearity + Interaction — possible leakage
findings.observation: Suspect features: ['h7_d48'].  These have Impact z-score > 3.0 but Non-linearity and Interaction z-scores both ≤ −0.5 — meaning they dominate the output through a purely linear path.
findings.why_it_matters: Leaked features (label-derived, near-target proxies) typically have very high Impact AND very low curvature (the model just memorises the linear shortcut). Genuine drivers usually accumulate some non-linearity or interactions during training.
findings.recommendation: Audit these features for post-hoc derivation from the target. Run an ablation: drop the feature, re-train, and verify the model still meets its metric goals.

findings.name: capacity
findings.headline: Healthy archetype distribution
findings.why_it_matters: No archetype dominates — the model has a diverse mix of feature roles.

findings.name: co_sensitivity
findings.headline: Co-Sensitivity refused to recommend any prune (2 groups found, but no prune-safe group)
findings.observation: k=2 functional groups, silhouette=0.55, permutation p=0.000, bootstrap ARI=0.59, best group NC fraction=5%.  Abort triggered because: no group has >50% Noise Candidates (best=5%).
findings.why_it_matters: Co-Sensitivity clusters features by gradient similarity. It only recommends pruning when a whole group is noise-dominated (>50%) AND the clustering itself is statistically supported. Refusing to prune is the SAFE outcome — the alternative is removing useful features.
findings.recommendation: Pruning is not warranted from this run. If you need to compress the model, use magnitude-based or movement pruning instead.
